metadata.title: Why Your Character Doesn't Fall Through the Floor
metadata.color_semantics: claude fidelity: INK #3D3929; SPARK #D97757 one accent.
metadata.author: Seth Brown
tags: Why Your Character Doesn't Fall Through
metadata.note: HAI Fellows batch v2 — Teardown register, @NikBearBrown, Remotion animated bookends. Credit: Seth Brown — Humanitarians AI Fellow. HONESTY: all numbers trace to source article.

beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The 12 principles describe how real bodies move, not how a cartoon style should feel. Removing those physical properties and faking them back in is where trouble starts. Inverse kinematics can pop, producing sudden unnatural jumps in the pose. Cleanup, catching and smoothing those artifacts, is the actual animation job.

artifactLines: The 12 principles describe how real bodies move, not how a cartoon style should feel.
Removing those physical properties and faking them back in is where trouble starts.
Inverse kinematics can pop, producing sudden unnatural jumps in the pose.
Cleanup, catching and smoothing those artifacts, is the actual animation job.

beats.narration_text: The point that the 12 principles describe how bodies actually move, and IK can pop and needs cleanup, is what I want to get my hands on. I want to understand why my character stays on the floor. Walk me through it. Give me: (1) which of the 12 principles map to real physical properties versus stylistic feel, (2) exactly what causes an IK pop and how the cleanup fixes it, (3) a small rig test I could run to see the fake physics fail.
